mnemonics: Go Geth Parity, Just Like A Net Besu (Imagine clients as a net catching blocks: Geth, Parity, Nethermind, Besu)
keyPoints: Ethereum has multiple client software implementations (e.g., Geth, Nethermind, Besu) written in different languages.
Clients are categorized as execution clients (for transaction/block processing) and consensus clients (for the Proof-of-Stake mechanism).
Releases follow a naming convention of cities and are planned via network upgrades (hard forks) rather than a traditional versioned software model.
commonMistakes: Believing there is only one 'official' Ethereum client (Geth is just one of several).
Assuming a 'release' is a software update for a single client, when it is actually a network-wide protocol upgrade that all clients must implement.
